_description: ESPN team/player stats for France (3 group + R32) and Paraguay (3 group + R32), R16 FRA vs PAR 2026-07-04

notes: Perfect record, 4W, 13 goals for / 2 against. SOT trending UP as tournament progresses: 8,5,9,12 -- season-high (12 SOT) came in their toughest test yet (Sweden, R32). Good regime coverage: their best output came against their best opponent, not their worst -- unlike Canada's untested-against-a-superior-side blind spot from the R16 match two days ago.
First-goal timing has real variance: 66', 14', 7', 45' -- they don't always start fast, meaning there IS a realistic window for an early Paraguay goal in some scenarios.
Mbappe: goals 2,2,0,2 (scored in 3/4 games, blanked only vs Norway where he had 2 assists instead). SOT 4,3,2,4.

Dembele: goals 0,1,3,0 (hat-trick vs Norway). SOT bimodal: 0,2,3,0 -- hit 2+ SOT only in the 2 games against weaker/mid opposition (Iraq, Norway); blanked on SOT vs the 2 tougher opponents (Senegal, Sweden).
Olise: 0 goals in all 4 games but assists 1,2,0,2 -- elite creator, present in 3/4 games via assist alone.

The ultimate giant-killer story: beat Turkey away with 21.5% possession, then held Germany to a 1-1 draw in regulation (forcing ET, eventually winning 5-4) in their R32 match -- the most direct, recent, relevant precedent for today's even-more-lopsided matchup.

Led at some point in 2 of 4 games (50%), including very recently vs a strong European side (Germany) for 12 minutes before being pegged back. This is a genuine team trait -- clinical, opportunistic finishing (a long-range effort vs Turkey, a header vs Germany) despite very low shot volume.

Team SOT floor is genuinely low and consistent (1,2,2 in groups, 3 in the R32 game which partly reflects extra-time action) -- unlike Canada's case, this IS well-regime-tested: Paraguay's low output persisted whether dominated territorially (TUR 21.5% poss, GER 24.4% poss) or in a balanced game (AUS). Real evidence, not an untested assumption.

Enciso: SOT 0,0,0,1 -- broke his SOT duck for the first time in the Germany R32 game (also scored there). IMPORTANT: a near-identical 'Enciso 1+ SOT' suppression call was made for GER-PAR and LOST -26.3 RBP specifically because the team's whole attacking ceiling broke through in that match. Do not over-anchor to the 0,0,0 pattern this time.

Almiron: 0 goals, 0 assists, 0 SOT in every game he's featured (missing entirely from the Australia squad list). Total attacking anonymity across all 4 games regardless of context (blowout loss, upset win, wild R32 draw) -- this is a personal-zero signal independent of game state, unlike the team-level Enciso case.
